What makes a good unit test? ✅

A good unit test is fast, isolated, and deterministic.

Key properties:

  • Fast — runs in milliseconds, no I/O or network
  • Isolated — tests one thing, no shared state
  • Readable — clearly expresses intent (Arrange / Act / Assert)
  • Reliable — same result every run, no flakiness

Give me a bad example 😬

 
// Bad: tests multiple things, relies on external state
test('user', async () => {
  const user = await db.find(1);         // network dep
  expect(user.name).toBe('Alice');       // test 1
  expect(user.email).toContain('@'));    // test 2
  expect(sendWelcomeEmail(user)).toBe(true); // side effect
});
